nodes.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**`vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE` fallback is currently bypassed by the new default**

Line 30 makes `inputs.auto_merge` always non-empty (`"false"`), so Line 242 never reaches `vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE`. That means repo-level opt-in (`vars...=true`) won’t work when caller omits `auto_merge`.

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
       auto_merge:
         description: "Whether to auto-merge 7-green PRs"
         type: string
         required: false
-        default: "false"
+        default: ""
...
-          SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE: ${{ inputs.auto_merge != '' && inputs.auto_merge || vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE || 'false' }}
+          SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE: ${{ inputs.auto_merge != '' && inputs.auto_merge || vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE || 'false' }}
```
</details>

 


Also applies to: 242-242

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In @.github/workflows/skeptic-cron-reusable.yml at line 30, The
inputs.auto_merge default of "false" forces a non-empty string so the workflow
never falls back to vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE; to fix, remove the hardcoded
default or set inputs.auto_merge to an empty string (default: "") and ensure the
runtime check compares the string value to "true" (e.g. use inputs.auto_merge ==
'true' or fall back to vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE when inputs.auto_merge is
empty) so repo-level opt-in via vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE works as intended.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- 4e71b3a2 -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commit ce86579

Fixed in commit ca2b8855: auto_merge default changed from "false" to "" (empty string). The expression on line 242 now properly falls through to vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE when caller omits auto_merge. The merge guard also now normalizes the value and only allows merge for explicit "true" or "1".

`@jleechan2015`, confirmed — the fix looks correct. Setting `default: ""` ensures `inputs.auto_merge` is empty when omitted, allowing the expression `inputs.auto_merge != '' && inputs.auto_merge || vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE || ''` to properly fall through to the repo variable. The fail-closed normalization in the merge guard (trim + lowercase, only accepting `"true"` or `"1"`) is also a good hardening improvement. Thanks for the detailed commit note!

<!-- <review_comment_addressed> -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Honor the repo variable when the input is omitted**

In this reusable workflow, `inputs.auto_merge` is populated with the default value when the caller omits the input, so after the default above it is the non-empty string `false`. GitHub Actions treats non-empty strings as truthy in `&&`/`||` expressions, which means this expression returns `false` before ever consulting `vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE`; callers using the thin template with only `SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE=true` set will still skip every 7-green merge. Please distinguish an omitted input from an explicit value, for example by using an empty input default or otherwise checking the variable before the defaulted input.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Fixed: auto_merge default changed from "false" to "" (empty string) so the vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE fallback expression in line 242 is actually reached when caller omits auto_merge. Commit ca2b8855.

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Normalize auto-merge input before enabling merges**

When `auto_merge` is omitted and the repository variable is set to a common false-like value such as `False`, `FALSE`, `off`, or has surrounding whitespace, this line passes that raw string through; the later merge guard only skips exact `"false"` or `"0"`, so those values will be treated as enabled and 7-green PRs can be auto-merged unexpectedly. Since this PR makes `vars.SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE` the opt-in control for callers, the workflow should fail closed by normalizing/allowlisting only explicit `true`/`1` values before the merge step.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Fixed: merge guard now normalizes SKEPTIC_CRON_AUTO_MERGE to lowercase + trimmed, and only allows merge when value is explicitly "true" or "1". All other values (False, FALSE, off, empty) skip merge — fail-closed. Commit ca2b8855.
